name: sharp-edges
description: Identify error-prone APIs and dangerous configurations
capabilities: Identify APIs with complex parameter requirements that can easily lead to errors
Detect dangerous configuration settings that bypass security controls
Recognize error-prone patterns in code that could lead to bugs
Assess risk in API design choices and recommend safer alternatives
Provide guidance on proper resource management for complex APIs
Spot timing and concurrency issues in API usage
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation/guide for identifying error-prone APIs and dangerous configurations. The static analyzer detected hardcoded URLs (lines 4, 70) which are legitimate source references to the original GitHub repository - not data exfiltration. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections at lines 3, 22, and 39 are false positives - the scanner misidentified text patterns as cryptographic issues when there are no cryptographic algorithms present. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: Code reviews often miss error-prone APIs and dangerous configurations that lead to bugs and security vulnerabilities. This skill helps identify these sharp edges before they cause production issues.
userTitle: Identify Dangerous APIs and Risky Configurations
limitations: This is a guide skill that provides methodology, not automated scanning
Does not execute code or perform actual security scans
Cannot detect all possible API vulnerabilities - requires human judgment
Knowledge is limited to common patterns documented in the skill

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers and developers performing code reviews
useCases.title: Security Audit Assistance
useCases.description: Use during code reviews to identify potentially dangerous API usage and risky configurations that could lead to security vulnerabilities.

useCases.title: API Design Review
useCases.description: Evaluate proposed API designs for known pitfalls and error-prone patterns before implementation.

useCases.title: Configuration Safety Check
useCases.description: Review configuration files and settings for dangerous defaults that could expose systems to risk.

promptTemplates.title: Basic API Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the sharp-edges skill to identify any error-prone APIs or dangerous configurations in this code snippet. Focus on APIs with non-obvious failure modes or complex parameter requirements.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a quick review of code for known problematic patterns

promptTemplates.title: Security Configuration Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the sharp-edges skill to analyze these configuration settings. Identify any defaults that are insecure or settings that could bypass security controls.
promptTemplates.scenario: When reviewing configuration files for security risks

promptTemplates.title: API Design Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the sharp-edges methodology, evaluate this proposed API design. What sharp edges should developers be aware of? What safer alternatives exist?
promptTemplates.scenario: When designing or reviewing new APIs before implementation

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Risk Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a thorough sharp-edges analysis on this codebase. Identify error-prone patterns, dangerous configurations, and provide risk assessments with recommendations for each finding.
promptTemplates.scenario: When performing a comprehensive security review

bestPractices: Always document identified sharp edges with clear explanations of the risk
Provide concrete examples of both incorrect and correct usage patterns
Recommend specific safer alternatives when available, with code examples
Keep sharp edge documentation updated as new vulnerabilities are discovered
antiPatterns: Ignoring API documentation warnings about known failure modes
Using default configurations without reviewing security implications
Assuming all APIs behave consistently across different contexts
Skipping resource management checks for APIs that require cleanup

faq.question: What is a sharp edge in code?
faq.answer: A sharp edge is an API or configuration that has non-obvious failure modes, complex requirements, or can lead to bugs and security issues if not used carefully. Examples include APIs with timing sensitivities, unclear error handling, or dangerous default settings.

faq.question: Does this skill scan my code automatically?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides methodology and knowledge for identifying sharp edges. It guides you through the review process but does not perform automated static or dynamic analysis of your code.

faq.question: Can this skill detect all security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: No. This skill helps identify common error-prone APIs and dangerous configurations, but it cannot detect all possible vulnerabilities. It should be used as part of a comprehensive security review process.

faq.question: What types of APIs does this skill help identify?
faq.answer: The skill helps identify APIs with complex parameter requirements, non-obvious failure modes, timing or concurrency issues, unclear error handling, and APIs requiring careful resource management.

faq.question: How is this skill different from static analysis tools?
faq.answer: Static analysis tools automatically scan code for patterns. This skill provides guidance and methodology for manual review, helping you understand WHY certain patterns are problematic and how to address them.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. The sharp-edges methodology is language-agnostic. The skill provides general principles for identifying problematic APIs and configurations across different programming languages and frameworks.
